prose:
Fukui reactivity indices computed for aniline (14 atoms: N, 6×C, 7×H) using DFT at tier 'standard' = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF, density-fit RI), gas phase, charge 0/multiplicity 1 for the neutral; charge +1/mult 2 for cation; charge −1/mult 2 for anion. Geometry taken directly from the user-supplied aniline.xyz (no re-optimization). Partial charges via Mulliken scheme (PySCF). All three SCFs converged; charge conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000). Integrity: trustworthy = true. No warnings.

Headline result — most nucleophilic site: Atom 1 (N), f⁻ = 0.20452359. Most electrophilic site: Atom 3 (ortho-C), f⁺ = 2.2975867. The ortho (atoms 3,4) and para (atom 7) carbons show the largest f⁺ and dual descriptor values, consistent with the well-known ortho/para-directing nature of the −NH₂ group in electrophilic aromatic substitution.

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard [aniline.xyz] --out aniline_fukui.json. Plot: agent_call_01_plot.png.
